name: The one principle that the Prophet and his followers unreservedly stood for was that
introduction:
God alone should be served. Does that call for dispute and quarrel? If anyone, it is the
Muslims who are entitled to quarrel with the Jews and Christians, for it is they rather than
the Muslims who insist that others beside God should also be made objects of service and
adoration.
An alternative translation of this portion could be: 'Would you then dispute with us for the
sake of Allah?' In this case the meaning of the verse would be that if their dispute was
sincere, it was easy to put an end to it by, agreeing to serve none but God.
